quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of misogyny and casteism moved into the board today, bringing with it a torrent of vile comments.

A front of slut-shaming remarks led the charge, with one anon claiming Handoo mothers 'slut out' their daughters. This sparked a downpour of replies. pouring in on the topic.

A high-pressure ridge of racism and caste comparison developed later in the day, as an anon claimed the Chinese have their own caste system.

This thread., with many weighing in on the similarities and differences between the two systems. Meanwhile, a cold snap of criticism hit the board as an anon BJP critic got shut down. responding to the criticism.

A scattered drizzle of violent and misogynistic remarks continued throughout the day, including a call for a bounty on a woman's head and a debate over beef consumption among Hindus.

These threads were brief but persistent, with 21 and 20 posts respectively.

articles.title: Anon Claims Handoo Mothers 'Slut Out' Daughters
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread claiming that Handoo mothers are trying to relive their youth through their daughters by 'slutting them out'.

Another anon responded by drawing a parallel, stating that just as some fathers force their sons to focus on studies because they themselves never had the opportunity, some mothers make their daughters do things they always wanted to do.

One respondent suggested that Handoo individuals should tell their mothers that after marriage, they will cover their heads with a pallu, not be their daughter-in-law. Another anon joked that gobbars should wear burkhas to prevent them from spreading their 'smell'.

A different anon commented that 'Chads will score stacies while incels get mad at things that are beyond their control'.

Another poster mocked a user saying they seemed to have been 'coomed' and couldn't figure out who was the mother and who was the daughter. Anon also claimed that Handoo 'mard' can't have a say in things without getting humiliated.

Another respondent expressed hopelessness, stating that they are 'out of control now' and 'nobody can stop it'. A different user made a baseless claim that Handoo 'mard' secretly enjoys seeing their daughters in revealing clothes.

Other comments included a suggestion that the Islamification of Hindus is the only way to save them from Islam and a remark blaming Modi for making India a 'gynocentric shithole'.

One user made a generalised statement that any woman with tattoos is 'without a doubt a whore'.

articles.title: Anon Claims Chinese Have Their Own Caste System
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying they just learned that Chinese have their own version of the caste system.

One respondent argued that the caste system in India is not what it used to be, citing that there are now thousands of castes.

They mentioned that during the British Raj, many tribes and peoples were mainstreamed and classified by the British. Another poster brought up the point that if someone's ancestors were low-caste, would they still hate their own family members.

They also asked if the poster would be arrogant if they were on the opposite end of the hate. One anon claimed that the caste system is imaginary and that stories of oppression were created to divide Hindus.

They argued that Hindus are ignoring the atrocities of the mullahs.

A different poster pointed out that if there was no social mobility, then how would one explain the success of certain castes that aren't part of any varna system. They mentioned Kayasthas as an example.

A respondent also mentioned that the Hukou system in China, which bans rural Chinese people from migrating to urban centers, is similar to the caste system in India.

They argued that if only India had a system half as effective. Anon also claimed that the Chinese caste system and Indian caste system are not comparable.

They argued that genetics play a role in determining tribe and caste.

articles.title: Anon BJP Critic Gets Shut Down
articles.body:
Anon launched a thread with an insult against the BJP.

"Ayy BJPoo madarchod. how will you defend this ?" Anon responded with a claim that a woman, presumably a public figure, supports Pakistan and liberal causes, and that BJP supporters are "brainwashed by teli modi media." Another Anon chimed in, accusing the woman of using her father's "looted money" and being a "digital dasi" who supports "pakis & palestine" and has relationships with men from those regions.

One respondent told the OP to stop being "obssessed" with the woman, saying "More you seethe more she will do randi things." Anon fired back at the OP, calling them a "simp jeet" who contributes to "Hoeflation" with their reactions.

The discussion took a turn when Anon brought up the woman's dietary habits, with one poster saying "problem ? only bhakts and sanghis seethes ovr someone's dietary habits lol." Anon expressed frustration with the BJP supporters, saying they "cannot give me a Genuine answer" and are "shifting the narrative." The thread continued with various insults and personal attacks, with one Anon suggesting that the woman's parents being jailed and her funding for foreign expenses being cut would not bother her.

The tone of the discussion remained aggressive and mocking throughout.

articles.title: Anon calls for bounty on woman
articles.body:
Anon proposed a 1 crore rupee bounty for cutting and bringing back body parts of a woman, suggesting those who do so be granted amnesty and a lump sum.

Another anon suggested self-immolation to 'cleanse the planet of parasites.' Some anons made unprovoked sexual and violent comments towards the woman.

One claimed she was related to the BJP and targeted 'hindoo taxpayers.' Others joked about her appearance. The thread included numerous abusive and threatening posts.

articles.title: Anon debates beef consumption among Hindus
articles.body:
Anon shared links to tweets by Shakti Bhat on the board. One anon noted that Hindu views on beef may shift as younger generations grow up, suggesting a potential increase in beef consumption among Hindus in the next decade.

Another anon countered that non-vegetarian diets, including beef, are not staples in many Indian regions, describing beef-eating habits in some areas as involving low-quality buffalo meat.

An exchange ensued over hygiene reasons for dietary choices, with one anon stating that some avoid pork for cleanliness reasons.
